title: EMILIA Standards Observatory
scope: Revision-aware standards cartography. Curated claims are source-locked; broad recon entries are discovery leads only.

methodology.review_model: A broad, correlated agent-assisted sweep followed by source-by-source adversarial reverification. Agent count is not reviewer independence.
methodology.publication_rule: Only records with a locked primary source, verified excerpt, operative-status basis, and curator rationale drive the public guarantee matrix.
methodology.absence_rule: A missing feature is recorded as no only when the locked artifact explicitly excludes it or a bounded source review found no mechanism; otherwise it is unknown.
methodology.correction_policy: Source authors may challenge any classification. Corrections preserve the prior snapshot and state the reason for the change.

dimensions.label: Named human
dimensions.question: Does the artifact bind an accountable human through a defined confirmation ceremony?

dimensions.label: RP-pinned acceptance
dimensions.question: Does the relying party supply the trust roots and sufficiency rule rather than accepting presenter-selected policy?

dimensions.label: Exact-action binding
dimensions.question: Does the evidence cryptographically bind the exact consequential action?

dimensions.label: Distinct-human quorum
dimensions.question: Does it define M-of-N approval by distinct accountable humans?

dimensions.label: Initiator exclusion
dimensions.question: Does it prevent the action initiator from approving its own action?

dimensions.label: One-time consumption
dimensions.question: Does it prevent a valid authorization from clearing execution more than once?

dimensions.label: Offline verification
dimensions.question: Can an unrelated relying party verify the artifact without contacting its issuer?

sources.title: Agent Protocols BoF Draft Charter (merged text)
sources.operative_basis: This file is linked as the draft charter from the current BoF materials; the older charter.md is historical precedent, not governing text.
sources.defines: Framework-level confirmation and evidence requirements, plus a way to request confirmation before an agent operation.
sources.limits: The operative merge does not retain a standalone confirmation-evidence deliverable or define a portable artifact.

quote.text: including the confirmation and evidence requirements for AI agent operations

named_human.rationale: User confirmation is in scope, but no ceremony or accountable-human binding is defined.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: The charter does not settle the relying-party trust model.

exact_action.rationale: Confirmation is requested for operations about to be performed, without a normative action-binding artifact.

distinct_quorum.rationale: No distinct-human quorum work item appears in the operative charter.

offline_verification.rationale: Independent or offline artifact verification is not required by the operative text.

sources.title: Agent Protocols Original Charter Proposal
sources.operative_basis: The text remains in the repository but was superseded by Charter_Merge.md for the BoF charter discussion.
sources.defines: A proposed standalone protocol for human confirmation of agent operations with cryptographic attestation.
sources.limits: It is not the operative merged charter and does not specify the evidence object or verification algorithm.

quote.text: It must provide cryptographic attestation of what the user has confirmed

named_human.rationale: It requires human confirmation but leaves identity and ceremony undefined.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: No relying-party acceptance model is defined.

exact_action.rationale: It covers operations but does not define canonical action binding.

offline_verification.rationale: Cryptographic attestation is required, but independent offline verification is not specified.

sources.title: Building the Trust Layer for Agentic Payments with AP2 and Verifiable Intent
sources.operative_basis: Current FIDO Alliance description of the AP2 and Verifiable Intent contribution.
sources.defines: Portable evidence of user authorization for agentic payments, composed with AP2 mandates.
sources.limits: The published description is payment-specific and does not claim EP's distinct-human quorum or initiator-exclusion profile.

quote.text: transforms user authorization into portable, verifiable evidence

named_human.rationale: It binds user authorization, while the public description does not define a named-human enrollment profile.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: Independent validation is explicit; the complete relying-party pin model is not described on this page.

exact_action.rationale: The framework is presented as evidence of user-authorized agent actions in payments.

distinct_quorum.rationale: No distinct-human M-of-N mechanism is described.

one_time_consumption.rationale: The public description does not establish execution consumption semantics.

offline_verification.rationale: The page explicitly claims portable evidence and independent validation without proprietary logs.

sources.title: AP2 Checkout Mandate
sources.defines: A merchant-signed checkout payload and checkout_hash used to identify the exact checkout in AP2 flows.
sources.limits: The checkout hash identifies an AP2 object; it does not by itself establish semantic equivalence to a CAID action or a general-purpose human ceremony.

quote.text: checkout_hash is the base64url-encoded hash of the value of checkout_jwt

named_human.rationale: AP2 composes user mandates, but the checkout object itself is merchant-signed.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: Validation is signature-based; general cross-framework acceptance is outside this object.

exact_action.rationale: checkout_hash binds the serialized checkout JWT value.

distinct_quorum.rationale: No distinct-human quorum is defined for this mandate.

one_time_consumption.rationale: This page does not establish cross-executor one-time consumption.

offline_verification.rationale: The signed JWT and hash can be verified without an AP2 issuer status assertion.

sources.title: WIMSE Workload Credentials
sources.operative_basis: Latest Datatracker revision of the active WIMSE working-group draft at snapshot time.
sources.defines: Credentials representing workload identity, with proof of possession handled by companion mechanisms.
sources.limits: It authenticates software workloads; it does not define named-human authorization for a consequential action.

quote.text: This document focuses on the credentials alone, independent of the proof-of-possession mechanism

named_human.rationale: The subject is a software workload, not an accountable approving human.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: Workload trust domains supply verification roots, not a complete action-evidence sufficiency bar.

exact_action.rationale: The credential binds workload identity, not one exact action.

initiator_exclusion.rationale: No action initiator/approver separation is defined.

one_time_consumption.rationale: PoP replay guidance is not authorization consumption.

offline_verification.rationale: Credentials can be cryptographically checked, subject to trust-domain key discovery and freshness.

sources.title: Condition-Bounded Credentials for Workload and Agent Identity
sources.operative_basis: Latest Datatracker revision at snapshot time; individual submission discussed in WIMSE.
sources.defines: Hardware-rooted possession under current attested conditions, with independent failure classes for possession, delegation, and authorization.
sources.limits: The draft explicitly says it is an authentication input and delegates human authorization to a separate offline-verifiable receipt joined on an action digest.

quote.text: This profile is an authentication input, not an authorization one

named_human.rationale: The draft explicitly leaves named-human authorization to a separate mechanism.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: It defines a verifier contract for condition evidence, not the complete authorization sufficiency policy.

exact_action.rationale: The composition text joins layers on a shared action digest; this profile's own possession proof is not the authorization.

initiator_exclusion.rationale: No human separation-of-duties rule is defined.

one_time_consumption.rationale: Key-use conditions and grant replay controls do not define once-only action authorization consumption.

offline_verification.rationale: Condition evidence is verifier-facing; the separate human receipt is described as offline-verifiable.

sources.title: SCITT Reference APIs
sources.operative_basis: Datatracker reports the working-group document in the RFC Editor queue at snapshot time.
sources.defines: HTTP resources for registering SCITT Signed Statements and obtaining and validating transparency receipts.
sources.limits: SCITT proves registration and transparency properties; payload-specific human authorization semantics belong to a Signed Statement profile.

quote.text: registration of Signed Statements, the issuance and validation of Receipts

named_human.rationale: SCRAPI is payload-agnostic and does not define a human authorization claim.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: Clients verify transparency-service keys; payload acceptance remains profile-specific.

exact_action.rationale: A Signed Statement may bind an action, but SCRAPI does not require that payload semantic.

distinct_quorum.rationale: No human authorization quorum is defined.

one_time_consumption.rationale: Transparency registration is not execution consumption.

offline_verification.rationale: Receipts remain verifiable while the relevant public key is retained.

sources.title: An Architecture for Auditing AI Agent Delegation and Interactions
sources.operative_basis: Latest Datatracker revision at snapshot time.
sources.defines: An audit architecture containing Interaction, Action, Delegation, and Authorization Transition Records, including HITL transitions.
sources.limits: The concrete Authorization Transition Record encoding remains an open work item and the examples explicitly use placeholder fields.

quote.text: The exact field names, claim names, and encodings shown here are placeholders

named_human.rationale: Human approvals and signed grants are auditable record inputs; no ceremony artifact is defined.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: The architecture names auditors and recorders without a complete acceptance profile.

exact_action.rationale: Action and transition records are linked, while concrete binding fields remain open.

distinct_quorum.rationale: No distinct-human authorization quorum is specified.

one_time_consumption.rationale: Audit records do not enforce once-only execution.

offline_verification.rationale: Independent recorders and receipts are contemplated; the complete record profile remains open.

sources.title: Agent Operation Authorization
sources.operative_basis: Latest Datatracker revision at snapshot time.
sources.defines: JWT claims and phases for verifiable delegation of fine-grained agent operations from human principals.
sources.limits: It carries user-intent provenance but does not define EP's device-bound named-human ceremony, distinct quorum, or initiator-exclusion profile.

quote.text: ensure cryptographic verification of user intent

named_human.rationale: Human principal and intent are central; a device-bound accountable-human ceremony is not defined.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: JWT verification is defined, while acceptance depends on deployment policy.

exact_action.rationale: The framework carries fine-grained operation authorization and operation proposals.

distinct_quorum.rationale: No distinct-human M-of-N authorization is defined.

initiator_exclusion.rationale: No general initiator-exclusion predicate is defined.

one_time_consumption.rationale: JWT replay controls exist, but a cross-executor once-only action consumption contract is not the core artifact.

offline_verification.rationale: The signed JWT claims are independently verifiable against configured keys.

sources.title: CHEQ: Confirmation AI Agent Decisions with Human in the Loop
sources.operative_basis: Datatracker marks revision 00 expired; retained because it directly competes for confirmation scope.
sources.defines: A human-confirmation protocol and signed CHEQ object designed to compose with agent protocols.
sources.limits: The expired draft does not define distinct-human quorum, initiator exclusion, or a durable execution-consumption service.

quote.text: The protocol must provide cryptographic assurances that the human being approves the action

named_human.rationale: A human confirmation is authenticated and cryptographically signed.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: Identity-provider verification is defined; broader relying-party evidence policy is not.

exact_action.rationale: The CHEQ memorializes the action the human confirms.

distinct_quorum.rationale: No M-of-N human approval is defined.

one_time_consumption.rationale: Replay protection is a requirement, but durable action-level consumption is not specified as EP does.

offline_verification.rationale: A signed object is defined, but the draft centers a protocol exchange and identity-provider authentication.

sources.title: Permit Receipts for Permit-Before-Commit Authorization
sources.operative_basis: Latest Datatracker revision at snapshot time.
sources.defines: A verifier contract and semantic fields for machine policy permits before external effects.
sources.limits: It deliberately does not choose a mandatory wire format and does not define a named-human confirmation ceremony.

quote.text: does not select a mandatory wire format

named_human.rationale: The receipt attests a policy permit, not human authorization.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: The verifier evaluates a selected verification profile and configured issuer evidence.

exact_action.rationale: The permit binds an effect request and action digest.

initiator_exclusion.rationale: No human initiator/approver separation is defined.

one_time_consumption.rationale: Anti-replay state is part of the verifier contract, but human authorization consumption is not.

offline_verification.rationale: The semantics support independent verification, while the mandatory portable wire profile remains open.

sources.title: Signed Decision Receipts for Machine-to-Machine Access Control
sources.operative_basis: Latest Datatracker revision at snapshot time.
sources.defines: Portable signed records of machine-to-machine access-control decisions with offline verification.
sources.limits: The receipt records a policy-engine decision rather than named-human approval, and nonce-based replay protection is optional.

quote.text: Receipts include an issued_at timestamp but do not include a nonce or sequence number

named_human.rationale: The signer is the access-control decision issuer, not an accountable approving human.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: Issuer keys are verified; a broader acceptance profile remains deployment policy.

exact_action.rationale: Receipts carry action references and decision context.

one_time_consumption.rationale: The base receipt omits nonce or sequence state; optional extensions do not establish the base guarantee.

offline_verification.rationale: The draft explicitly defines independently verifiable portable receipts.

sources.title: Delegation Receipt Protocol for AI Agent Authorization
sources.operative_basis: Latest Datatracker revision at snapshot time.
sources.defines: Receipts recording delegated authority, chain narrowing, risk checks, replay detection, and optional offline verification.
sources.limits: It establishes delegation from a principal; that is not identical to a fresh device-bound human approval ceremony for each action.

named_human.rationale: A human principal is identified, but per-action accountable-human ceremony semantics are not EP's profile.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: Verification and trust inputs are defined, with offline restrictions for some receipts.

exact_action.rationale: Delegation receipts bind delegated operations and optional tool outputs, not necessarily a fresh exact-action approval.

distinct_quorum.rationale: No distinct-human M-of-N authorization is defined.

initiator_exclusion.rationale: No general initiator-exclusion rule is defined.

one_time_consumption.rationale: Replay detection records receipt and session use; semantics differ from action-digest-keyed execution consumption.

offline_verification.rationale: Offline mode exists but is conditional on receipt properties.

sources.title: Agent Route Origin Authorization
sources.operative_basis: Latest Datatracker revision at snapshot time.
sources.defines: A cryptographic route and policy envelope for agent actions, including approval state and an optional reference to an approval artifact.
sources.limits: approval_artifact_ref is a literal socket: it references a signed approval artifact without defining that artifact's human ceremony semantics.

quote.text: Reference to the signed approval artifact

named_human.rationale: Approval state and an artifact reference exist, but the human artifact is undefined.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: Policy envelope verification is defined; approval-artifact acceptance remains external.

exact_action.rationale: The route authorization and evidence record bind the action path and enforcement outcome.

initiator_exclusion.rationale: No human self-approval exclusion is defined.

one_time_consumption.rationale: A replay-prevention cache exists, but the referenced human artifact has no consumption contract here.

offline_verification.rationale: The framework is designed for independent after-the-fact verification.

sources.title: Authorization Receipts for High-Risk Agent Actions
sources.operative_basis: Latest posted Datatracker revision at snapshot time; staged local revisions are excluded.
sources.defines: A named-human, exact-action authorization receipt with Class-A WebAuthn ceremony, separation of duties, offline verification, and one-time use.
sources.limits: The artifact does not prove the action was wise, lawful, safe, or successfully executed; deployment still needs complete mediation.

quote.text: binds a named, accountable human approver to one exact high-risk action

named_human.rationale: The core artifact binds a named accountable human through an enrolled ceremony.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: The relying party pins approver, policy, audience, issuer, and freshness inputs.

exact_action.rationale: Every signoff and receipt binds the canonical exact action.

distinct_quorum.rationale: The base artifact supports one approver; distinct-human M-of-N is supplied by EP-QUORUM.

initiator_exclusion.rationale: The trust-receipt verification model enforces separation of duties.

one_time_consumption.rationale: A one-time nonce and consumption rule prevent authorization reuse.

offline_verification.rationale: The receipt is designed for full offline verification against pinned keys.

sources.title: Multi-Party Quorum Authorization for High-Risk Agent Actions
sources.operative_basis: Latest posted Datatracker revision at snapshot time.
sources.defines: Distinct-human M-of-N and ordered approval trails composed from fresh, device-bound ceremonies over one exact action.
sources.limits: Quorum verifies authorization evidence; it does not establish the substantive correctness of the approved action or eliminate credential-enrollment risk.

quote.text: binds a set of distinct accountable humans

named_human.rationale: Every member is an accountable human under an enrolled signoff profile.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: The relying party pins the quorum policy, audience, roles, and approver directory.

exact_action.rationale: Every member signs the same exact action hash.

distinct_quorum.rationale: The profile defines threshold and ordered quorums of distinct humans.

initiator_exclusion.rationale: Each approving human must be distinct from the initiator.

one_time_consumption.rationale: Member signoffs and the composed decision retain once-consumable semantics.

offline_verification.rationale: The complete quorum predicate is offline-verifiable.

sources.title: Authorization Evidence Chains
sources.operative_basis: Latest posted Datatracker revision at snapshot time; staged -03 is not treated as public status.
sources.defines: Typed same-action composition of heterogeneous receipts under a relying-party requirement, producing an offline fail-closed decision.
sources.limits: The chain is only as trustworthy as its registered component verifiers and pinned acceptance inputs; it does not turn machine policy into human authorization.

quote.text: checks that every component binds one canonical action digest

named_human.rationale: Built-in EP human legs require a receipt or distinct-human quorum under pinned profiles.

rp_pinned_acceptance.rationale: ALLOW requires the relying party's out-of-band requirement and expected action.

exact_action.rationale: Every component must bind the same executor-expected canonical action digest.

distinct_quorum.rationale: The ep-quorum built-in verifies the exact pinned distinct-human policy.

initiator_exclusion.rationale: The built-in quorum verifier enforces initiator exclusion.

one_time_consumption.rationale: The offline object composes evidence; stateful one-time execution is enforced by the companion gate.

offline_verification.rationale: The composition algorithm and component checks run offline.

operative_conflicts.title: Historical charter mistaken for operative charter
operative_conflicts.finding: The original file contains a standalone confirmation-evidence deliverable; the operative merged charter does not.
operative_conflicts.consequence: The standards move changes from citing an existing deliverable to proposing restoration of a modular work item.

events.title: Agenda request submitted for the WIMSE and EMILIA two-row composition
events.description: An external WIMSE participant submitted a request for a short IETF 126 slot covering possession in the live channel plus action-bound authorization evidence joined by digest.
events.evidence: User-supplied screenshot of a message addressed to the WIMSE chairs and public list. The archive URL was not yet discoverable when this snapshot was built.
events.truth_boundary: This records a request, not agenda acceptance, working-group adoption, or IETF endorsement.

events.title: Condition-Bounded Credentials advances to revision 01
events.description: The revision explicitly separates authentication from delegation and human authorization, then describes conjunctive composition on a shared action digest.
events.evidence: Locked IETF archive text for revision 01.
events.truth_boundary: Individual Internet-Draft discussed in WIMSE; not a working-group-adopted document.

events.title: WIMSE architecture key-management issue 85 closes
events.description: The architecture issue titled Workload Identity Credentials key management is understated was closed as the credential lifecycle discussion advanced.
events.truth_boundary: Issue closure is a repository event, not proof of consensus on every proposed lifecycle model.

events.title: WIMSE receives a two-hour IETF 126 session
events.description: The IETF Secretariat scheduled the WIMSE session for 20 July 2026 in Vienna.
events.truth_boundary: The session is scheduled; individual presentation requests remain subject to the chairs' agenda decisions.

events.title: SCRAPI reaches the RFC Editor queue
events.description: The current Datatracker state places draft-ietf-scitt-scrapi in the RFC Editor queue, materially later than the earlier IESG-evaluation snapshot.
events.truth_boundary: The document is not an RFC until publication; its state can continue to change.

frontiers.title: CAID Action-Mapping Profile
frontiers.problem: A signed crosswalk proves who asserted that two native digests co-refer; it does not prove the source objects carry materially equivalent action semantics.
frontiers.deliverable: A profile-bounded mapping record that binds both source artifacts, the mapping profile and version, normalized material fields, information loss, translator identity, and the relying party's accepted mapper set.
acceptance_tests: Two independent translators derive the same material-field projection.
A missing required field yields INDETERMINATE, never equivalence.
A lossy currency, amount, destination, or purpose mapping is refused.
An AP2 checkout_hash is recomputed from a real checkout_jwt before comparison.
The mapping result binds the exact profile hash and both source-byte digests.

frontiers.title: Modular Confirmation-Evidence Work Item
frontiers.problem: The operative agentproto charter mentions confirmation and evidence but no longer has the original standalone artifact deliverable.
frontiers.deliverable: Mechanism-neutral charter language requiring a portable operation-bound artifact independently verifiable by a relying party.
acceptance_tests: The work item is explicit rather than inferred from framework prose.
A boolean asserted by the confirming service cannot satisfy the requirement alone.
The charter avoids legal non-repudiation claims and does not select EMILIA as the mechanism.

frontiers.title: WIMSE Two-Row Composition Profile
frontiers.problem: WIMSE proves the live workload possesses its protected key; a relying party still needs separate evidence that a named human authorized this exact operation.
frontiers.deliverable: A runnable profile composing condition-bounded possession and human-authorization evidence under one relying-party rule, joined on an executor-computed action identifier.
acceptance_tests: Authorization cannot substitute for live holder binding.
A valid pair over different actions is refused.
Each row preserves its own issuer, freshness, and failure reason.

recon.claim_boundary: Aggregate counts only. The per-artifact recon index is held privately and never published; entries are discovery leads, not publication-grade claims, and do not drive the guarantee matrix. Individual efforts are named publicly only after promotion into the source-locked 17-source matrix.
